questions.text: A simple example of a dot product is the
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Force is a vector quantity that represents the influence that causes an object to undergo a change in motion. While it can be related to work, it is not an example of a dot product itself.
Energy is a scalar quantity that measures the capacity to do work. However, it is not directly the result of a dot product between two vectors.
Work is defined as the dot product of force and displacement vectors. It quantifies the energy transferred when a force acts on an object, making it a direct example of a dot product.
Momentum is a vector quantity that represents the product of an object's mass and velocity. It does not involve the dot product and is not a scalar.

questions.explanation: Work done is defined as a dot product of force and distance travelled in the direction of applied force.
